channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The RAM shortage could get even worse if Samsung labor protests cut production
item.description: The RAM shortage caused by demand from AI datacenters is already driving up prices on phones, PS5s, and Raspberry Pis, but it could be about to get even worse. Samsung is facing employee protests...

item.title: Top 6 Cryptocurrency Data APIs: Comprehensive Comparison (2026)
item.description: Most developers building crypto tools make the same mistake. They grab the first API with a free tier, get it working, then six months later realize they're juggling three providers, paying for...

item.title: How Project Maven taught the military to love AI
item.description: In the first 24 hours of the assault on Iran, the US military struck more than 1,000 targets, nearly double the scale of the "shock and awe" attack on Iraq over two decades ago. This acceleration...

item.title: Tesla (TSLA) discloses $2B AI hardware company acquisition buried
item.description: Article URL: https://electrek.co/2026/04/23/tesla-tsla-quietly-discloses-2-billion-ai-hardware-acquisition-10q/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47892765 Points: 5 # Comments: 2

item.title: Unpacking Git 2.54: Key Updates and Their Impact on Tech Startups
item.description: Introduction In the rapidly evolving world of software development, the tools we use to manage our projects can significantly affect efficiency and collaboration. Git, the ubiquitous version...

item.title: Combining Microsoft AGT Policies with Waxell Observability: A Reference Architecture
item.description: This post is for teams that have made two decisions: Use Microsoft's Agent Governance Toolkit for policy enforcement. Need observability, cost tracking, and collaboration on top of that. We'll...

item.title: TCP worked, UDP silently died: shipping a DNS proxy to fly.io
item.description: I shipped v0.2.0 of dnsink — a Rust DNS proxy with threat-intelligence feeds and DNS tunneling detection — to fly.io this week. TCP worked on the first deploy. UDP silently dropped every reply....

item.title: Marked-up Mac minis flood eBay amid shortages driven by AI
item.description: Apple’s sold-out Mac mini is spawning marked-up eBay listings as demand surges for the compact desktop, now favored for running local AI models and tools.

item.title: DeepSeek V4 Price: Pro vs Flash API Costs
item.description: DeepSeek V4 pricing is split across two API models: deepseek-v4-pro and deepseek-v4-flash. The official pricing page lists separate rates for cache-hit input, cache-miss input, and output tokens....

item.title: I built a modern plumber business website using an AI agent workflow (Google Antigravity) looking for feedback
item.description: I built a modern plumber business website using an AI agent workflow (Google Antigravity) looking for feedback Hi everyone, I’ve been learning full-stack development and recently built a complete...

item.title: Hi!
item.description: Olá comunidade tudo bem? Me chamo Matheus e vim para a comunidade para ensinar e aprender junto programação, espero que eu continue firme e que daqui alguns anos possa participar de palestras como...

item.title: Xreal’s best AR glasses are $599 for good now
item.description: The Xreal One Pro are the company’s best AR glasses, touting thin optics that keep out reflections so you can enjoy a crisp, contrast-rich image of whatever content you’re watching. Xreal has...

item.title: The Meta Security Layer: What Nobody Told You About Zero-Human Companies
item.description: I open-sourced a little thing called mesa. It's an orchestrator, paperclip alternative — you run it on your laptop, it spawns coding agents, babysits them, wires them up to your actual work. Local...

item.title: 360-degree cameras have a new superpower
item.description: Imagine Google Street View, except you can walk around like it's a video game. Now imagine you don't need to wait for Google to come film because it's completely DIY. Insta360, the leading maker...

item.title: Tesla’s Cybercab goes into production — so why is Musk tapping the brakes?
item.description: Tesla's Cybercab is now in production at the company's Gigafactory in Austin, Texas, but Elon Musk is sounding unusually cautious about the rollout. The robotaxi's start of production was...

item.title: Apple's Next CEO Needs to Launch a Killer AI Product
item.description: Tim Cook was a great CEO, but he didn’t crack AI. It’s job number 1 for John Ternus.

item.title: AirPods, Touch Bars, and the rest of Tim Cook’s legacy
item.description: We knew at some point Tim Cook would step down from his position as Apple's CEO. Over the last year, it has become increasingly obvious that John Ternus was his likely successor. The news this...

item.title: NASA Employees Duped in Chinese Phishing Scheme Targeting U.S. Defense Software
item.description: The Office of Inspector General (OIG) of the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) has revealed how a Chinese national posed as a U.S. researcher as part of a spear-phishing...

item.title: Uber CTO Praveen Neppalli Naga joins stacked StrictlyVC SF lineup for April 30 event

item.title: The Trump phone still isn’t real
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. We're back to being ignored, and the phone seems...

item.title: I don’t think Gwyneth Paltrow knows what a peptide is

item.title: Nothing introduces an AI-powered dictation tool
item.description: Nothing's new on-device dictation tool supports over 100 languages.

item.title: DeepSeek previews new AI model that ‘closes the gap’ with frontier models
item.description: DeepSeek says both models are more efficient and performant than DeepSeek V3.2 due to architectural improvements, and have almost "closed the gap" with current leading models, both open and...

item.title: A new Republican privacy bill could be ‘worse than no standard at all’
item.description: Congress is once again attempting to pass a national data privacy law. But while it would introduce new protections in some states, it would weaken privacy rights in others - and it's missing...

item.title: The Download: supercharged scams and studying AI healthcare
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. We’re in a new era of AI-driven scams When ChatGPT was...

item.title: In another wild turn for AI chips, Meta signs deal for millions of Amazon AI CPUs
item.description: Meta has commandeered a big chunk of Amazon's homegrown CPUs (not GPUs) for AI agentic workloads, signaling that a new kind of chip race has begun.

item.title: Musk vs. Altman is here, and it’s going to get messy
item.description: Elon Musk cofounded OpenAI, and then flounced off in a huff when he wasn't anointed CEO, leaving Sam Altman as the last power-hungry man standing. Now, Musk is back with a lawsuit, and a trial is...

item.title: Bridging the AI Agent Authority Gap: Continuous Observability as the Decision Engine
item.description: The AI Agent Authority Gap - From Ungoverned to Delegation As discussed in our previous article, AI agents are exposing a structural gap in enterprise security, but the problem is often framed too...

item.title: 26 FakeWallet Apps Found on Apple App Store Targeting Crypto Seed Phrases
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a set of malicious apps on the Apple App Store that impersonate popular cryptocurrency wallets in an attempt to steal recovery phrases and private keys...
